questions.question: Define synchronization in Java and explain its importance in multithreaded programming.
keyPoints: Synchronization is a mechanism to control thread access to shared resources
Prevents data corruption and ensures thread safety
Uses synchronized keyword

questions.question: Explain the difference between synchronized methods and blocks in Java. Provide an example of each.
keyPoints: Synchronized method: entire method locked on the current object (this)
Synchronized block: lock on a specified object, providing finer granularity
Only one thread can hold the lock at a time
Example code demonstrating synchronized method and block
Explanation of the monitor/lock concept in Java

questions.question: Demonstrate a race condition in a multithreaded Java program and explain how to fix it using synchronization.
keyPoints: Shared resource (e.g., counter) accessed by multiple threads without synchronization
Race condition resulting in incorrect output
Solution using synchronized method or block to ensure thread safety
Correct output after applying synchronization
Explanation of how synchronization prevents data corruption

questions.question: List the benefits and trade-offs of using synchronization in Java.
keyPoints: Benefits: thread safety, data integrity, prevention of data corruption
Trade-offs: performance overhead, potential for deadlocks

questions.question: Explain the concept of a monitor in Java and its role in synchronization.
keyPoints: Definition of a monitor in Java
Every object in Java has an intrinsic lock (monitor)
Role of the monitor in synchronization: controlling access to shared resources

questions.question: Explain the difference between wait(), notify(), and notifyAll() methods in Java.
keyPoints: wait(): causes the current thread to wait until another thread invokes notify() or notifyAll()
notify(): wakes up a single thread that is waiting on the object's monitor
notifyAll(): wakes up all threads that are waiting on the object's monitor
Example code demonstrating the use of wait(), notify(), and notifyAll() methods

questions.question: Explain the concept of deadlocks in Java and provide an example.
keyPoints: Definition of a deadlock in Java
Example code demonstrating a deadlock scenario
Explanation of how to avoid deadlocks in Java

questions.question: Explain the concept of starvation in Java and provide an example.
keyPoints: Definition of starvation in Java
Example code demonstrating a starvation scenario
Explanation of how to avoid starvation in Java
